reasoning: The resolution dates occurred in late 2025 and early 2026, which are now in the past. To resolve YES, ACLED would need to record >105 incidents of "Violence against civilians" per month (more than 10x the reference baseline of 10.5). While Venezuela experienced significant political repression, kidnappings, and arbitrary arrests following the 2024 elections, ACLED categorizes most of this as state repression or excessive force at protests, not necessarily separate "Violence against civilians" events. Reports summarizing 2025 violence (like the OHCHR and Human Rights Watch world reports) indicate casualties and arrests in the dozens or low hundreds across several months, which is far below the threshold of 105 distinct violent attacks on civilians in a single 30-day window. There was no civil war or massive outbreak of daily lethal violence. Given the dates have passed with no such extreme spike reported, the probability of exceeding the threshold is exceptionally low for all periods.
